segments.text: Assistant: Hello, this is Preston Community Centre.
Man: Oh, hello.
Man: My name's Andrew Shepherd.
Man: I'm ringing about the classes you offer.
Assistant: OK, Mr. Shepherd.
Assistant: Are there any classes in particular that you're interested in?
Man: Yes, you've got a class called Photography, I believe?
Assistant: That's right.
Assistant: It's the first time we offer this class because many people enquired about it last term.
Assistant: It'll be held in a library not far from our community centre.
Man: Right.
Man: I know it's on the weekend, right?
Assistant: Yes, it's on Saturday, and it will occupy the whole morning.
Man: OK.
Man: Um, what do I need to bring?
Assistant: Obviously, you have to bring your own camera.
Assistant: Some people bring a lot of accessories like an extra lens, but there's really no need for this class.
Assistant: It's mainly focusing on composition really, and getting the most out of the basic camera.
Man: That's exactly what I want.
Man: OK, then I've heard there's a class on cooking, right?
Assistant: Yes, it's called French cooking class.
Assistant: As the name suggests, it will teach you cooking skills in French cuisine.
Man: Oh, great.
Man: Where will that be held?
Assistant: This class is special, so we arrange it in a kitchen where we can get the necessary cooking equipment.
Assistant: Let me see, yes, the Anita Kitchen.
Assistant: As for the time of the course, it used to be on the second Tuesday of every month, but there's a small adjustment this time.
Assistant: Although it's still held on a monthly basis, the exact time will be changed to the first Tuesday.
Man: Well, actually it's my wife who's really interested, and I think it's a good fit for her because our daughter has to take a guitar lesson every Tuesday, so my wife is available.
Assistant: That's good.
Assistant: But I have to remind you that aprons are not provided in this class, so when you do come, please bring one with you in case you get dirty when cooking.
Assistant: What's more, don't forget to bring some money with you, as the ingredients for cooking, like vegetables or meat, have to be paid by yourself.
Man: OK, useful information to know.
Man: Then there's another class that my wife would like to join – that's the Beading class.
Assistant: Oh, I've taken the class myself.
Assistant: The tutor is excellent.
Assistant: Last term it was held in a tennis club, which is going to be redecorated soon, so we plan to change this class to a golf club this time.
Assistant: And it'll be held on the evening of every Wednesday.
Man: Do I have to bring the beads with me?
Assistant: Yes, that's the only material needed for this class.
Man: OK, I think this class is also a good choice for her.
Man: Um, do you have any other classes that I can join?
Assistant: If you like, I think you can take our Painting class.
Assistant: It's a popular class, so this term we're going to move it to a high school so that we get more room.
Man: What time will it be?
Assistant: It'll be on every Monday evening.
Man: The time is good for me.
Man: Do I have to bring the paints?
Assistant: Well, paints will be provided by the tutor, I know that.
Assistant: Um, the information says you'll just need some brushes.
Man: I see.
Man: OK, just another question about the Beading class.
Man: I've just remembered my wife asked me to find out the exact time – when will the first class begin?
Assistant: Well, the deadline for registration is April 30th, and we are not going to start the class until May 15th.
Man: OK.
Man: What's the fee for this class?
Assistant: It was 95 dollars last term, but the cost will be a little more this term.
Assistant: Well, let me check the details.
Assistant: OK, it'll be 100 dollars this term, just 5 dollars more.
Man: Sounds reasonable.
Man: Then about the location.
Man: It's in the club, which room exactly?
Assistant: Room J52.
Man: OK, just one more question.
Man: Is parking convenient?
Assistant: I'm sorry to tell you that parking space is only available for the club's premier members.
Assistant: But if you drive, there's a parking lot opposite the club, just beside the station.
Assistant: You need to pay only a small parking fee.
Man: OK.
Man: That's all I want to know.
Man: I'll call you later after I discuss it with my wife.
Man: Thank you very much.
Assistant: You're welcome.
Assistant: Bye!
Man: Bye!
